title: Sleeper Solvers: Budgeting a Node-Expansion Portfolio
strategies: trivial: dump the whole budget onto the first-listed solver, ignoring the probes entirely

greedy: read the value at the LAST probe checkpoint for every solver, commit the entire budget to whichever currently looks best (the obvious single-armed recipe -- falls for the fast-saturating decoy on trap instances)

strong: fit an extrapolated final-value estimate per solver from the probe's successive increments (accelerating trend -> optimistic compounding extrapolation; decelerating trend -> saturating-tail extrapolation), rank by that estimate, and split the budget across the top few ranked solvers with decaying weights so a correctly-flagged late bloomer still gets the majority share while a wrong call doesn't zero out the runner-up
invalid: allocate far more node-expansions than the budget allows, violating sum(alloc) <= budget
